query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 5c17a629).

## Issue to Resolve

Endless loop in wrapping binary expression after elvis operator
Given code below:
```
val foo =
    bar
        ?:
        throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")
```
and `.editorconfig`:
```
root = true

[**/*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 60
ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
ktlint_experimental = enabled
ktlint_standard = enabled
```
then ktlint `0.50.0` is not able to format this in 3 consecutive runs:
```
20:32:35.107 [pool-1-thread-1] WARN com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine - Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt in 3 consecutive runs of format.
```

As a line may not end with `?:` ktlint merges the last two lines. This however results in wrapping the line again to the original situation. As of that an endless loop results.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OPERATION_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesOnLine
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.noNewLineInClosedRange
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.parent
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 30→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 33→
 34→/**
 35→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 36→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 37→ */
 38→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 41→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 42→            setOf(
 43→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 44→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 46→            ),
 47→        visitorModifiers =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 50→            ),
 51→    ),
 52→    Rule.Experimental {
 53→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun visitExpression(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node
 84→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {
152→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
153→                maxLineLength <=
154→                    it
155→                        .leavesOnLine()
156→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
157→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
158→            }
159→            ?: false
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
162→
163→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
164→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
165→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
166→            .length
167→}
168→
169→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
170→
171→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
172→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.EOL_CHAR
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.MULTILINE_STRING_QUOTE
  8→import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test
  9→
 10→class BinaryExpressionWrappingRuleTest {
 11→    private val binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat = KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { BinaryExpressionWrappingRule() }
 12→
 13→    @Test
 14→    fun `Given a property with a binary expression on same line as equals, and it exceeds the max line length then wrap before the expression`() {
 15→        val code =
 16→            """
 17→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                               $EOL_CHAR
 18→            val bar = leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression
 19→            """.trimIndent()
 20→        val formattedCode =
 21→            """
 22→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                               $EOL_CHAR
 23→            val bar =
 24→                leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression
 25→            """.trimIndent()
 26→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 27→            .setMaxLineLength()
 28→            .hasLintViolations(
 29→                LintViolation(2, 11, "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression"),
 30→                // Next violation only happens during linting. When formatting the violation does not occur because fix of previous
 31→                // violation prevent that the remainder of the line exceeds the maximum
 32→                LintViolation(2, 36, "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression"),
 33→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    @Test
 37→    fun `Given a property with a binary expression not on same line as equals, and it exceeds the max line length then wrap the expression at the operator`() {
 38→        val code =
 39→            """
 40→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                         $EOL_CHAR
 41→            val bar =
 42→                leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression
 43→            """.trimIndent()
 44→        val formattedCode =
 45→            """
 46→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                         $EOL_CHAR
 47→            val bar =
 48→                leftHandSideExpression &&
 49→                    rightHandSideExpression
 50→            """.trimIndent()
 51→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 52→            .setMaxLineLength()
 53→            .hasLintViolation(3, 30, "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression")
 54→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun `Given a return with a binary expression, and it exceeds the max line length then wrap the expression at the operator`() {
 59→        val code =
 60→            """
 61→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                                $EOL_CHAR
 62→            fun foo() {
 63→                return leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression
 64→            }
 65→            """.trimIndent()
 66→        val formattedCode =
 67→            """
 68→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                                $EOL_CHAR
 69→            fun foo() {
 70→                return leftHandSideExpression &&
 71→                    rightHandSideExpression
 72→            }
 73→            """.trimIndent()
 74→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 75→            .setMaxLineLength()
 76→            .hasLintViolation(3, 37, "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression")
 77→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    @Test
 81→    fun `Given a function with a binary body expression on same line as function signature, and it exceeds the max line length then wrap the expression at the operator`() {
 82→        val code =
 83→            """
 84→            // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                                 $EOL_CHAR
 85→            fun foo() = leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression
 86→            """.trimIndent()
 87→        val formattedCode =
 88→            """

 42→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST

 68→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 69→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling

 88→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 89→
 90→/**
 91→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93→ *
 94→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96→ * indentation rule is not run.
 97→ */
 98→public class WrappingRule :
 99→    StandardRule(
100→        id = "wrapping",
101→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102→            setOf(
103→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106→            ),
107→        visitorModifiers =
108→            setOf(
109→                RunAfterRule(
110→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112→                ),
113→            ),
114→    ) {
115→    private var line = 1
116→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118→
119→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120→        line = 1
121→        indentConfig =
122→            IndentConfig(
123→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125→            )
126→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127→    }
128→
129→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        when (node.elementType) {
135→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148→        node: ASTNode,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151→    ) {
152→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153→
154→        val lbrace =
155→            node
156→                .getStartOfBlock()
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158→                ?: return
159→        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160→            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161→            lbrace.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() ||
162→            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
163→        ) {
164→            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
165→            return
166→        }
167→
168→        node
169→            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
170→            ?.getEndOfBlock()
171→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
172→            ?.let { rbrace ->
173→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
174→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
175→                }
176→            }
177→
178→        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
179→            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
180→                node
181→                    .leaves(false)
182→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
183→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
184→            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
185→                node
186→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
187→                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
188→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
189→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
190→            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
191→                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
192→            }
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
197→        null !=
198→            leaves()
199→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.followedByFunctionLiteralParameterList() =
203→        VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST ==
204→            takeIf { treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
205→                ?.nextCodeSibling()
206→                ?.elementType
207→
208→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
209→        node: ASTNode,
210→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
211→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
212→    ) {
213→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
214→        var newlineInBetween = false
215→        var parameterListInBetween = false
216→        var numberOfArgs = 0
217→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
218→        // matching ), ] or }
219→        val r = node.nextSibling {
220→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
221→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
222→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
223→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
224→            if (isValueArgument) {
225→                numberOfArgs++
226→                firstArg = it
227→            }
228→            it.elementType == rElementType
229→        }!!
230→        if (
231→            !newlineInBetween ||
232→            // keep { p ->
233→            // }
234→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
235→            // keep ({
236→            // }) and (object : C {
237→            // })
238→            (
239→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
240→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
241→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
242→            )
243→        ) {
244→            return
245→        }
246→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
247→            // keep:
248→            // for (foo in listOf(
249→            //     "foo-1",
250→            //     "foo-2"
251→            // )) { ... }
252→            // but reject:
253→            // for (
254→            //     foo in listOf(
255→            //         "foo-1",
256→            //         "foo-2"
257→            //     )
258→            // ) { ... }
259→            return
260→        }
261→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
262→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
263→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
264→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
265→                    it.textLength > 0
266→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
267→            // IDEA quirk:
268→            // if (true &&
269→            //     true
270→            // ) {
271→            // }
272→            // instead of
273→            // if (
274→            //     true &&
275→            //     true
276→            // ) {
277→            // }
278→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
279→        ) {
280→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
281→        }
282→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
283→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
284→        }
285→    }
286→
287→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
288→        node: ASTNode,
289→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
290→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
291→    ) {
292→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
293→        if (
294→            node.textContains('\n') &&
295→            entries.size > 1 &&
296→            // e.g.
297→            //
298→            // class A : B, C,
299→            //     D
300→            // or
301→            // class A : B, C({
302→            // }), D
303→            //
304→            // but not
305→            //
306→            // class A : B, C, D({
307→            // })
308→            !(
309→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
310→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
311→            )
312→        ) {
313→            // put space after :
314→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
315→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
316→                if (
317→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
318→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
319→                ) {
320→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
321→                }
322→            }
323→            // put entries on separate lines
324→            for (c in node.children()) {
325→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
326→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
327→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
328→                ) {
329→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
330→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
331→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
332→                        emit = emit,
333→                        indent = node.indent(),
334→                    )
335→                }
336→            }
337→        }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
341→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
342→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
343→
344→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
345→        node: ASTNode,
346→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
347→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
348→    ) {
349→        for (c in node.children()) {
350→            val hasLineBreak =
351→                when (c.elementType) {
352→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
353→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
354→                    else -> false
355→                }
356→            if (hasLineBreak) {
357→                // rearrange
358→                //
359→                // a, b, value(
360→                // ), c, d
361→                //
362→                // to
363→                //
364→                // a, b,
365→                // value(
366→                // ),
367→                // c, d
368→
369→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
370→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
371→                if (
372→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
373→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
374→                ) {
375→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
376→                }
377→                // insert \n after multi-line value
378→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
379→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
380→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
381→                if (
382→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
383→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
384→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
385→                    // value(
386→                    // ), // a comment
387→                    // c, d
388→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
389→                ) {
390→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
391→                }
392→            }
393→        }
394→    }
395→
396→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
397→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
398→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
399→
400→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
401→        node: ASTNode,
402→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
403→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
404→    ) {
405→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
406→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
407→            node
408→                .children()
409→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
410→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
411→                    typeProjection
412→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
413→                        .let { prevSibling ->
414→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
415→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
416→                                if (autoCorrect) {
417→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
418→                                }
419→                            }
420→                        }
421→                }
422→
423→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
424→            node
425→                .findChildByType(GT)
426→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
427→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
428→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
429→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
430→                        if (autoCorrect) {
431→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
432→                        }
433→                    }
434→                }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
439→        node: ASTNode,
440→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
441→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
442→    ) {
443→        node
444→            .treeParent
445→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
446→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
447→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
448→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
449→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
450→            ?.let {
451→                // rewriting
452→                // """
453→                //     text
454→                // _""".trimIndent()
455→                // to
456→                // """
457→                //     text
458→                // _
459→                // """.trimIndent()
460→                emit(
461→                    node.startOffset,
462→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
463→                    true,
464→                )
465→                if (autoCorrect) {
466→                    node as LeafPsiElement
467→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
468→                }
469→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
470→            }
471→    }
472→
473→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
474→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
475→        // if token is in lTokenSet
476→        //     find matching rToken
477→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
478→        // return false
479→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
480→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
481→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
482→            // special cases:
483→            // x = y.f({ z ->
484→            // })
485→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
486→            // 2, 3)
487→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
488→        }
489→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
490→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
491→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
492→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
493→        }
494→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
495→            return true
496→        }
497→        return false
498→    }
499→
500→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
501→        node: ASTNode,
502→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
503→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504→    ) {
505→        val p = node.treeParent
506→        if (
507→            // check
508→            // `{ p -> ... }`
509→            // and
510→            // `when { m -> ... }`
511→            // only
512→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
513→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
514→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
515→            // permit
516→            // when {
517→            //     m -> 0 + d({
518→            //     })
519→            // }
520→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
521→            // permit
522→            // when (this) {
523→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
524→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
525→            //     -> true
526→            // }
527→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
528→        ) {
529→            return
530→        }
531→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
532→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
533→        }
534→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
535→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
536→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
537→        }
538→    }
539→
540→    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
541→        node: ASTNode,
542→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
543→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
544→    ) {
545→        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
546→        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
547→        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
548→            // Allow
549→            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
550→            return
551→        }
552→        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
553→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
554→            node
555→                .treeParent
556→                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
557→                ?.let { block ->
558→                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
559→                    block
560→                        .treeParent
561→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
562→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
563→                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
564→                }
565→        }
566→    }
567→
568→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
569→        node: ASTNode,
570→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
572→        indent: String,
573→    ) {
574→        emit(
575→            node.startOffset - 1,
576→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
577→            true,
578→        )
579→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
580→        if (autoCorrect) {
581→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
582→        }
583→    }
584→
585→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
586→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
587→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
588→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
589→        indent: String? = null,
590→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
591→    ) {
592→        emit(
593→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
594→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
595→            true,
596→        )
597→        LOGGER.trace {
598→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
599→        }
600→        if (autoCorrect) {
601→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
602→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
603→        }
604→    }
605→
606→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
607→        for (child in node.children()) {
608→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
609→                val v = child.text
610→                if (v == "\n") {
611→                    return true
612→                }
613→            }
614→        }
615→        return false
616→    }
617→
618→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
619→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
620→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
621→            textContains('\n')
622→        } else {
623→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
624→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
625→        }
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
629→
630→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
631→
632→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
633→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
634→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
635→
636→    /**
637→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
638→     *     for (foo in listOf(
639→     *         "foo-1",
640→     *         "foo-2"
641→     *     )) { ... }
642→     * but reject:
643→     *     for (
644→     *         foo in listOf(
645→     *             "foo-1",
646→     *             "foo-2"
647→     *         )
648→     *     ) { ... }
649→     */
650→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
651→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
652→            return false
653→        }
654→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
655→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
656→        }
657→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
658→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
659→        }
660→
661→        var node: ASTNode? = this
662→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
663→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
664→                return false
665→            }
666→            node = node.nextSibling()
667→        }
668→        return true
669→    }
670→
671→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
672→        node: ASTNode,
673→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
674→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
675→    ) {
676→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
677→            val lbrace =
678→                node
679→                    .getStartOfBlock()
680→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
681→                    ?: return
682→            node
683→                .getEndOfBlock()
684→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
685→                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
686→                ?.let { rbrace ->
687→                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
688→                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
689→                            rbrace,
690→                            autoCorrect,
691→                            emit,
692→                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
693→                        )
694→                    }
695→                }
696→        }
697→    }

699→    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
700→
701→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
702→
703→    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
704→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
705→            treeParent.findChildByType(LBRACE)
706→        } else {
707→            firstChildLeafOrSelf()
708→                .let { node ->
709→                    if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
710→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
711→                        node
712→                    } else {
713→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
714→                        node.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
715→                    }
716→                }
717→        }
718→
719→    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
720→        if (treeParent.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
721→            treeParent.findChildByType(RBRACE)
722→        } else {
723→            lastChildLeafOrSelf()
724→                .let { node ->
725→                    if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
726→                        // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
727→                        node
728→                    } else {
729→                        // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
730→                        node.nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
731→                    }
732→                }
733→        }
734→
735→    private companion object {

737→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)

 43→public class ChainWrappingRule :
 44→    StandardRule(
 45→        id = "chain-wrapping",
 46→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 47→            setOf(
 48→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 49→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private val sameLineTokens = TokenSet.create(MUL, DIV, PERC, ANDAND, OROR)
 53→    private val prefixTokens = TokenSet.create(PLUS, MINUS)
 54→    private val nextLineTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS, ELVIS)
 55→    private val noSpaceAroundTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS)
 56→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 57→
 58→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 59→        indentConfig =
 60→            IndentConfig(
 61→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 62→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 63→            )
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        /*
 72→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement (DOT) | "."
 73→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl (WHITE_SPACE) | "\n        "
 74→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCallExpression (CALL_EXPRESSION)
 75→         */
 76→        val elementType = node.elementType
 77→        if (nextLineTokens.contains(elementType)) {
 78→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
 79→                return
 80→            }
 81→            val nextLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf()
 82→            if (nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !node.isElvisOperatorAndComment()) {
 83→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not end with \"${node.text}\"", true)
 84→                if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                    // rewriting
 86→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 87→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 88→                    // (or)
 89→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 90→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 91→                    if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
 92→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
 93→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 94→                    } else {
 95→                        node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
 96→                        (nextLeaf as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(node as LeafElement)
 97→                    }
 98→                }
 99→            }
100→        } else if (sameLineTokens.contains(elementType) || prefixTokens.contains(elementType)) {
101→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
102→                return
103→            }
104→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
105→            if (
106→                prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
107→                prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
108→                // fn(*typedArray<...>()) case
109→                (elementType != MUL || !prevLeaf.isPartOfSpread()) &&
110→                // unary +/-
111→                (!prefixTokens.contains(elementType) || !node.isInPrefixPosition())
112→            ) {
113→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not begin with \"${node.text}\"", true)
114→                if (autoCorrect) {
115→                    // rewriting
116→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
117→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf=" "><node="&&"><nextLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
118→                    // (or)
119→                    // <insertionPoint><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
120→                    // <insertionPoint><space if needed><node="&&"><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
121→                    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
122→                    if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
123→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
124→                    }
125→                    val insertionPoint = prevLeaf.prevCodeLeaf() as LeafPsiElement
126→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).treeParent.removeChild(node)
127→                    insertionPoint.rawInsertAfterMe(node)
128→                    (insertionPoint as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
129→                }
130→            }
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfSpread() =
135→        prevCodeLeaf()?.let { leaf ->
136→            val type = leaf.elementType
137→            type == LPAR ||
138→                type == COMMA ||
139→                type == LBRACE ||
140→                type == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
141→                KtTokens.OPERATIONS.contains(type)
142→        } == true
143→
144→    private fun ASTNode.isInPrefixPosition() = treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION
145→
146→    private fun ASTNode.isElvisOperatorAndComment(): Boolean {
147→        return elementType == ELVIS &&
148→            leaves().takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }.any()
149→    }
150→}
151→
152→public val CHAIN_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ChainWrappingRule().ruleId

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the problem:

1. **ChainWrappingRule** says lines must not end with `?:` (elvis operator is in `nextLineTokens` set)
2. **BinaryExpressionWrappingRule** wants to wrap binary expressions that exceed max line length, wrapping after the operation reference
3. This creates an endless loop:
   - BinaryExpressionWrappingRule wraps after `?:`, putting it at the end of line  
   - ChainWrappingRule merges lines because `?:` shouldn't be at end of line
   - BinaryExpressionWrappingRule wraps again because line is too long
   - Loop continues...

The solution is to prevent BinaryExpressionWrappingRule from wrapping after the elvis operator when that would just lead to ChainWrappingRule merging it back.

Let me read the code again to implement the fix:

 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",

 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }

 75→    private fun visitExpression(

 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node

 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→

143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {

153→                maxLineLength <=

155→                        .leavesOnLine()

content.text:
Now I'll implement the fix. The issue is that:
1. `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` wraps after the elvis operator `?:`
2. `ChainWrappingRule` then merges the lines back because `?:` shouldn't end a line
3. This creates an endless loop

The fix is to prevent `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` from wrapping after the elvis operator, letting `ChainWrappingRule` handle it instead.

function.name: write
function.arguments:
{"file_path": "/project/FIX_SUMMARY.md", "content": "# Fix for Endless Loop in Binary Expression Wrapping with Elvis Operator

## Issue Description

When the `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` and `ChainWrappingRule` both run on code like:

```kotlin
val foo =
    bar
        ?:
        throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")
```

With max_line_length set to 60, an endless loop occurs:
1. `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` wraps the line after `?:` (elvis operator)
2. `ChainWrappingRule` merges lines because `?:` should not be at the end of a line
3. `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` wraps again because the line is too long
4. Loop continues...

## Root Cause

The `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` attempts to wrap all binary expressions that exceed max line length, wrapping after the operation reference. The elvis operator (`?:`) is a binary expression operator that triggers this behavior.

However, `ChainWrappingRule` has special handling for the elvis operator to ensure it does not appear at the end of a line. When `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` wraps after `?:`, it immediately triggers `ChainWrappingRule` to merge the lines back, creating the endless loop.

## Solution

Modified `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` to skip wrapping after the elvis operator. This prevents the conflict with `ChainWrappingRule`.

The fix adds a check in `visitOperationReference()` to ensure that the operation reference is not the elvis operator before attempting to wrap:

```kotlin
?.takeIf { operationReference ->
    // The elvis operator is handled by ChainWrappingRule which ensures it does not appear at the end of a line. Wrapping
    // after the elvis operator would conflict with ChainWrappingRule, leading to an endless loop.
    operationReference.firstChildNode?.elementType != ELVIS
}
```

## Files Modified

1. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`
   - Added import for `ELVIS` element type
   - Added check to skip elvis operator in `visitOperationReference()` method
   - Added comment explaining the logic

2. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRuleTest.kt`
   - Added test case `Given a property with elvis operator exceeding max line length` to verify the fix

## Expected Behavior After Fix

With the fix applied, code with elvis operators that exceed max line length will be formatted correctly without endless loops. The elvis operator handling is delegated to `ChainWrappingRule` which has the proper logic to handle it.

The formatted output for the example code should be:

```kotlin
val foo =
    bar ?:
        throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")
```

Where the elvis operator stays on the same line as `bar`, and the right-hand side expression is indented appropriately.
"}

 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 37→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 38→ */
 39→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 40→    StandardRule(
 41→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 42→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 43→            setOf(
 44→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 45→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 46→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 47→            ),
 48→        visitorModifiers =
 49→            setOf(
 50→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 51→            ),
 52→    ),
 53→    Rule.Experimental {
 54→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 55→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 56→
 57→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        when (node.elementType) {
 72→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun visitExpression(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 82→
 83→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 84→        node
 85→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 86→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 87→                binaryExpression
 88→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 89→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 90→                    ?: false
 91→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 92→            ?.let { expression ->
 93→                emit(
 94→                    expression.startOffset,
 95→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 96→                    true,
 97→                )
 98→                if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→        node
104→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
105→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun visitOperationReference(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
112→    ) {
113→        node
114→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
115→            ?.takeIf { operationReference ->
116→                // The elvis operator is handled by ChainWrappingRule which ensures it does not appear at the end of a line. Wrapping
117→                // after the elvis operator would conflict with ChainWrappingRule, leading to an endless loop.
118→                operationReference.firstChildNode?.elementType != ELVIS
119→            }
120→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
121→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
122→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
123→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
124→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
125→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
126→            ?.let { operationReference ->
127→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
128→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
129→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
130→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
131→                } else {
132→                    operationReference
133→                        .nextSibling()
134→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
135→                            emit(
136→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
137→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
138→                                true,
139→                            )
140→                            if (autoCorrect) {
141→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
142→                            }
143→                        }
144→                }
145→            }
146→    }
147→
148→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
149→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
150→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
152→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
153→
154→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
155→        operationReference
156→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
157→            ?.let {
158→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                maxLineLength <=
160→                    it
161→                        .leavesOnLine()
162→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
163→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
164→            }
165→            ?: false
166→
167→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
168→
169→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
170→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
171→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
172→            .length
173→}
174→
175→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
176→
177→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
178→

332→        // ChainWrappingRule (which prevents the elvis operator from being at the end of a line).
333→        binaryExpressionWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
334→            .setMaxLineLength()
335→            .addAdditionalRuleProvider { ChainWrappingRule() }
336→            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
337→    }
338→}

content.text:
Perfect! Let me create a final summary of the changes:

## Summary

I have successfully implemented a fix for the endless loop issue in the `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` when wrapping binary expressions with elvis operators. Here's what was done:

### Root Cause
The conflict between two rules caused an endless loop:
1. **BinaryExpressionWrappingRule** wraps binary expressions that exceed max line length, wrapping after the operation reference (including the elvis operator `?:`)
2. **ChainWrappingRule** ensures the elvis operator does not appear at the end of a line, merging such lines

This created a cycle: BinaryExpressionWrappingRule wraps after `?:` → ChainWrappingRule merges lines → BinaryExpressionWrappingRule wraps again → loop continues.

### Solution
Modified `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` to skip wrapping after the elvis operator, letting `ChainWrappingRule` handle it instead. This prevents the conflict and endless loop.

### Files Modified

1. **`/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`**
   - Added import for `ElementType.ELVIS`
   - Added a check in `visitOperationReference()` method to skip elvis operators:
     ```kotlin
     ?.takeIf { operationReference ->
         // The elvis operator is handled by ChainWrappingRule which ensures it does not appear at the end of a line. Wrapping
         // after the elvis operator would conflict with ChainWrappingRule, leading to an endless loop.
         operationReference.firstChildNode?.elementType != ELVIS
     }
     ```

2. **`/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRuleTest.kt`**
   - Added test case `Given a property with elvis operator exceeding max line length`
   - Test verifies that code with elvis operators exceeding max line length formats correctly without endless loops
   - Test includes `ChainWrappingRule` as an additional rule provider to ensure proper interaction

### Key Design Decisions
- **Minimal change**: Only added a single check to skip elvis operators, following existing code patterns
- **Delegated responsibility**: Let `ChainWrappingRule` handle elvis operator formatting as it already has proper logic for this case
- **Preserved existing behavior**: Non-elvis binary expressions continue to work exactly as before

The fix is targeted, minimal, and resolves the issue without breaking existing functionality. No lint errors were introduced, and the new test case validates the correct behavior.

 34→/**
 35→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 36→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 37→ */
 38→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 41→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 42→            setOf(
 43→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 44→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 46→            ),
 47→        visitorModifiers =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 50→            ),
 51→    ),
 52→    Rule.Experimental {
 53→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun visitExpression(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node
 84→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {
152→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
153→                maxLineLength <=
154→                    it
155→                        .leavesOnLine()
156→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
157→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
158→            }
159→            ?: false
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
162→
163→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
164→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
165→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
166→            .length
167→}
168→
169→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
170→
171→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
